availability.summary: A live GET probe for a known contract award id returned HTTP 200 JSON on 2026-05-11, confirming the award-detail route is currently callable.

sources.title: AwardRetrieveViewSet request parsing and category dispatch
Derived request coverage check for award detail endpoint
Reviewer report for award detail canonical award lookup alias
sources.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-2-canonical-award-id-alias for v2__awards__award_id

parameters.description: Single path identifier for the award profile request. Treat this request field as the endpoint's carrier for canonical_award_lookup_id: if you are chaining from v2__search__spending_by_award, pass generated_internal_id unchanged here even though this route names the field award_id. Documentation also says the endpoint accepts a generated award hash or an internal database id, and source code shows a fallback lookup against generated_unique_award_id_legacy when string lookup misses.

documented.notes: Docs show contract and assistance hash examples and say the endpoint accepts the generated hash or internal database id.

constraints: Required path segment.
Digit-only values are parsed as integer award ids; other values are treated as generated award identifiers.
When chaining from search or funding workflows, reuse canonical_award_lookup_id unchanged rather than substituting the human-facing Award ID label.
Current-profile evidence indicates the canonical route includes a trailing slash and preserves case.

observed.notes: A live 200 response confirmed a generated award hash works, and the reviewer-backed drilldown confirmed CONT_AWD_HT940216C0001_9700_-NONE-_-NONE- carried successfully from search generated_internal_id into this path. Numeric, legacy, and canonicalization variants remain supported by current-profile/source evidence.

templates.name: contract-award-by-generated-id
templates.description: Fetch one contract award profile using a generated award hash in the path.

templates.name: award-by-numeric-id
templates.description: Fetch one award profile using a numeric internal award id in the path.

response.shapeSummary: Single JSON object for one requested award, with shared award-summary fields plus category-specific procurement or assistance branches; a live 2026-05-11 contract probe returned the procurement branch with parent_award, latest_transaction_contract_data, PSC/NAICS hierarchies, and common financial totals.

fields.description: Internal USAspending numeric award identifier for the returned award profile. It can support some local lookups, but it is not the preferred cross-endpoint canonical_award_lookup_id when the generated award identifier is available.

Canonical generated award identifier echoed by the service. In this semantic bundle, normalize it as canonical_award_lookup_id: search may label the same business key generated_internal_id, this route accepts it as path award_id, and award funding expects the same value under request body field award_id.
constraints: Returned even when the request used a numeric internal id.
Use this echoed generated value as canonical_award_lookup_id when chaining into award funding or other award drilldowns.

observed.notes: Treat generated_unique_award_id as this endpoint's response label for canonical_award_lookup_id. Reviewer-backed drilldown evidence showed the same generated hash business key carried from search into award detail and then onward into award funding despite endpoint-specific field names.

fields.description: Award family used to determine which category-specific response branch is populated.
constraints: Branch parsing should check category before reading category-specific fields.

fields.description: Award subtype code within the returned category family.
Human-readable description of the award type.
Award narrative description derived from the award or latest transaction.
Procurement Instrument Identifier for contract and IDV awards.

documented.notes: Documented on contract and IDV response families only.

constraints: Usually absent for assistance awards.

fields.description: Financial-assistance award identifier for assistance responses.

documented.notes: Documented on financial-assistance response family only.

constraints: Usually absent for contract and IDV awards.

fields.description: Assistance URI / alternate assistance identifier when present.

documented.notes: Documented on financial-assistance response family only.

constraints: Usually absent for procurement awards.

fields.description: Award-level total obligation amount.
constraints: May be null for some assistance categories.

fields.description: Count of associated subawards.
Total dollars associated with subawards when available.
Agency object describing the awarding organization, including top-tier and subtier agency metadata and office name.
Agency object for the funding organization; source and current-profile evidence show it can be null for some assistance awards.

documented.notes: Financial-assistance docs mark the field nullable.

constraints: Handle null on assistance payloads.

fields.description: Recipient profile object with recipient identifiers, parent-recipient data, business categories, and nested recipient location fields.
Award performance period object. Contract and IDV responses can include potential_end_date, while assistance responses use the assistance variant of the structure.
constraints: Nested fields vary slightly by category.

fields.description: Nested place-of-performance location object for the award.
Large contract / IDV-only object with competition, pricing, PSC, NAICS, and other contract metadata from the latest transaction.
constraints: Present for contract and IDV branches, absent for assistance branches.

fields.description: Parent award / parent IDV metadata when the requested award has a parent relationship.
constraints: Null for assistance awards and for root contract/IDV awards without a parent.

fields.description: NAICS hierarchy for procurement awards when a NAICS code is available.
constraints: Procurement-only field.

fields.description: PSC hierarchy for procurement awards when a PSC code is available.
constraints: Procurement-only field.

fields.description: Record type code returned on financial-assistance payloads.
constraints: Assistance-only field.

fields.description: Assistance-only CFDA detail array with program metadata and funding totals by CFDA number.
constraints: Assistance-only field.

fields.description: Assistance-only funding opportunity object. The data-structure section misspells the field as funding_opportunty, while examples and source code use funding_opportunity.

documented.notes: Docs contain a field-name typo in the financial-assistance structure.

constraints: Assistance-only field.

fields.description: Sum of account-level outlays derived from DEFC / File C financial account rows associated with the award.
constraints: Different concept from total_outlay.

fields.description: Sum of account-level obligations derived from DEFC / File C financial account rows associated with the award.
constraints: Different concept from total_obligation.

fields.description: Per-DEFC outlay amounts associated with the award.
constraints: May be empty when no qualifying DEFC rows exist.

fields.description: Per-DEFC obligation amounts associated with the award.
constraints: May be empty when no qualifying DEFC rows exist.

fields.description: Executive compensation object with up to five officer entries.
Award-level outlay measure computed separately from DEFC account totals. Source code adds it to all response branches, but documentation does not describe it consistently across award families.

documented.notes: Documentation is uneven across response families.

constraints: Different concept from total_account_outlay.

observed.notes: Live contract payload included the field with a null value.

notes: The main MCP improvement here is semantic normalization: treat search generated_internal_id, this path award_id, and response generated_unique_award_id as the same canonical_award_lookup_id for cross-endpoint handoff.
The current raw profile already exposes the only documented request field, award_id.
The larger MCP need is semantic guidance: category-branch parsing, identifier-selection workflow, and documentation mismatches such as funding_opportunity naming and total_outlay presence.

contradictions.statement: The financial-assistance data structure misspells funding_opportunity as funding_opportunty, while the example payload and source code use funding_opportunity.
contradictions.impact: Callers should read funding_opportunity in actual responses and not hard-code the typo from the doc structure section.

contradictions.statement: Documentation does not describe total_outlay consistently across award families, but source code and the current raw profile indicate the field is returned more broadly.
contradictions.impact: Preserve total_outlay when present and do not confuse it with total_account_outlay.

quirks.statement: Digit-only award ids are parsed as integer award_id values, non-digit ids are treated as generated award identifiers, and source code retries generated_unique_award_id_legacy when the generated id lookup misses.
quirks.impact: Numeric ids, current generated ids, and some legacy generated ids may all resolve to the same endpoint.

quirks.statement: Current-profile evidence indicates the canonical route includes a trailing slash and may normalize some assistance request hashes before echoing generated_unique_award_id in the response.
quirks.impact: Clients should follow redirects and compare assistance identifiers against the returned generated_unique_award_id, not only the request path string.

quirks.statement: A live contract probe confirmed the route returns a single JSON object for the requested award rather than an array.
quirks.impact: Clients should parse one award object per call.

gaps.statement: The endpoint exposes a very large latest_transaction_contract_data object, but this first-pass semantic bundle does not enumerate every procurement subfield individually.
gaps.impact: Callers needing exhaustive contract-detail subfields should inspect the raw payload or expand this semantic bundle further.

risks.statement: The response schema changes materially by category: procurement awards use latest_transaction_contract_data, NAICS/PSC hierarchies, and parent-award procurement metadata, while assistance awards use CFDA and funding-opportunity sections.
risks.impact: Consumers must branch on category before assuming nested fields exist.

risks.statement: Related award drilldown endpoints relabel the same handoff business key; normalize it as canonical_award_lookup_id. When chaining from award search, use generated_internal_id as canonical_award_lookup_id and pass it unchanged as this endpoint's award_id rather than relying on the display Award ID.
risks.impact: Using one canonical alias reduces fragile cross-endpoint joins: search emits generated_internal_id, this route accepts award_id, and the response exposes generated_unique_award_id for reuse in funding or other award drilldowns.

risks.statement: Current-profile evidence indicates error payloads vary by route match, with JSON 404s for some unresolved numeric ids and HTML 404s for some malformed string ids.
risks.impact: Robust clients should inspect HTTP status and content type before assuming error bodies are JSON.
